title: typstR — Package Blueprint
text: typstR R package creating publication-ready scientific documents Quarto + Typst R-native workflow. try replace Quarto’s built-Typst support. Instead, builds top provides: opinionated scientific publication formats manuscript scaffolding metadata helpers institutional branding hooks reusable Typst templates convenience wrappers rendering validation

title: typstR — Package Blueprint
text: “polished R workflow scientific institutional PDF publishing Quarto Typst.”

title: typstR — Package Blueprint
text: Quarto already supports Typst output custom Typst formats, gap basic compatibility. gap cohesive, reusable, R-friendly package turns Quarto + Typst practical publication workflow. Existing related tools typr typstable show ecosystem real, provide complete manuscript framework.

title: typstR — Package Blueprint
text: researchers writing working papers economists policy analysts institutes producing reports briefs R users want LaTeX-quality PDFs cleaner authoring experience teams already use Quarto want polished PDF workflow

title: typstR — Package Blueprint
text: Quarto extension bundled inside R package one high-quality base Typst format one two publication presets manuscript scaffolding helpers metadata helpers render diagnostics examples templates

title: typstR — Package Blueprint
text: full journal submission compatibility many publishers direct import LaTeX .cls .sty low-level Typst parser universal conversion LaTeX Typst extensive HTML/Word output support

title: typstR — Package Blueprint
text: Provides helpers : - project creation - metadata generation - validation - render wrappers - utility functions notes, appendices, affiliations, etc.

text: Lives inside inst/ contains: - _extension.yml - format definitions - partials - template assets - bundled Typst template files

title: typstR — Package Blueprint
text: Contains reusable Typst building blocks : - title page - author affiliation block - abstract keywords - bibliography - table figure styling - appendix handling - institutional branding

text: title: “Trade Fragmentation Industrial Policy” subtitle: “Evidence Central Eastern Europe” author: - name: “Jane Doe” affiliation: “1” email: “jane@example.org” corresponding: true - name: “John Smith” affiliation: “2” affiliations: - id: “1” name: “Vienna Institute International Economic Studies” - id: “2” name: “University Somewhere” format: typstR-workingpaper: toc: true appendix: true logo: “logo.png” typstR: abstract: | paper studies… keywords: [“trade”, “industrial policy”, “CEE”] jel: [“F10”, “F13”, “L52”] acknowledgements: | thank… report-number: “WP 001” —

what: Start from the commented branding block
title: Customizing Branding
text: working paper starter ships commented fields: Uncomment fields need keep rest defaults.
code: typstR:   # logo: "logo.png"   # accent-color: "#0A5DA6"   # primary-font: "Linux Libertine"   # footer: "Working Paper Series"   # disclaimer-page: true

what: Add a logo and accent color
title: Customizing Branding
text: Use logo image path relative manuscript, accent-color main highlight color used format. usually enough align title page institute working paper series.

what: Change the primary font and footer
title: Customizing Branding
text: primary-font changes main text family, footer adds short text page footer. Use font available rendering environment. sharing project, document non-default font requirement alongside manuscript.
code: typstR:   primary-font: "Linux Libertine"   footer: "Working Paper Series"

title: Customizing Branding
text: can also set custom margins enable disclaimer page directly YAML. disclaimer-page useful series needs standard notice ahead main manuscript.
code: typstR:   margins:     x: 1.1in     y: 1in   disclaimer-page: true

what: Render only when Quarto is available
title: Customizing Branding
text: Branding changes take effect render time, example call stays non-evaluated vignette: output look right, inspect YAML field names first. common mistake using near miss accent_color instead accent-color. end--end onboarding flow, return vignette("getting-started", package = "typstR").

title: Getting Started
text: Start creating working paper directory: creates small project : template.qmd manuscript _quarto.yml project settings references.bib bibliography entries bundled typstR extension used template

what: Know the three files you will edit most
title: Getting Started
text: working-paper starter intentionally small, file distinct job: template.qmd manuscript edit day day _quarto.yml stores project-level Quarto settings references.bib add bibliography entries remember one thing vignette, remember normal flow template.qmd content, _quarto.yml project settings, references.bib citations.

title: Getting Started
text: working-paper template already contains main fields users need: title, authors, affiliations, abstract, bibliography wiring, typstR: block publication metadata. can also build structured metadata R pasting Quarto file:
code: typstR:   keywords:     - supply chains     - trade policy   jel:     - F14     - F13   report-number: "WP 001"   funding: |     This research was supported by the Applied Trade Policy Programme. lead_author <- author(   "Elena Fischer",   email = "elena.fischer@example.org",   corresponding = TRUE,   affiliation = "1" )  home_institute <- affiliation("1", "Centre for European Industrial Analysis")  manuscript_meta(   authors = list(lead_author),   affiliations = list(home_institute) ) #> <typstR_meta> 1 author(s), 1 affiliation(s)

title: Getting Started
text: generated _quarto.yml keeps project wiring minimal: usually need change day one, place look later want project-wide Quarto behavior. starter references.bib includes one example entry can see expected BibTeX shape immediately. Add entries keep bibliography: references.bib manuscript YAML.

title: Getting Started
text: Keep manuscript template.qmd. shipped starter uses compact working-paper structure: Introduction Data Methods Results Conclusion References Add citations references.bib cite manuscript standard Quarto syntax @smith2020.

title: Getting Started
text: Rendering depends Quarto, call shown run vignette checks. prefer guard call scripts, use condition package tests use:

title: Getting Started
text: basic workflow working, vignettes cover two main follow-tasks instead repeating scaffold/render introduction: vignette("working-papers", package = "typstR") working-paper metadata vignette("customizing-branding", package = "typstR") branding fields

title: Working Papers
text: Use author() affiliation() build structured metadata:
code: authors <- list(   author(     "Elena Fischer",     email = "elena.fischer@example.org",     corresponding = TRUE,     affiliation = "1"   ),   author(     "Martin Kovac",     affiliation = c("1", "2")   ) )  affiliations <- list(   affiliation("1", "Centre for European Industrial Analysis"),   affiliation("2", "University of Bratislava") )  manuscript_meta(authors = authors, affiliations = affiliations) #> <typstR_meta> 2 author(s), 2 affiliation(s)

title: Working Papers
text: working-paper template expects abstract ordinary Quarto front matter publication-specific fields inside typstR:. main working-paper difference article format: JEL codes report number usually belong title page.
code: abstract: |   This working paper studies how manufacturing firms adjust supplier networks   after trade policy shocks. typstR:   keywords:     - supply chains     - trade policy     - firm adjustment   jel:     - F14     - F13   report-number: "WP 001"

what: Reuse helper functions for standard statements
title: Working Papers
text: package includes small helpers common end-matter blocks: helpers useful want consistent structure across multiple papers series.
code: funding("This research was supported by the Applied Trade Policy Programme.") #> [1] "This research was supported by the Applied Trade Policy Programme." #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "typstR_funding" "character" data_availability("Replication files will be released after publication.") #> [1] "Replication files will be released after publication." #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "typstR_data_availability" "character" code_availability("Code is available from the corresponding author on request.") #> [1] "Code is available from the corresponding author on request." #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "typstR_code_availability" "character"

title: Working Papers
text: scaffolder already includes references.bib, usual workflow : add BibTeX entries references.bib keep bibliography: references.bib Quarto YAML cite sources body keys @smith2020 leave ## References end document

what: Use the starter template as the default shape
title: Working Papers
text: create_working_paper() gives compact manuscript skeleton : academic title block metadata bibliography wiring acknowledgements, funding, report number support room working-paper branding without editing Typst files directly full scaffold--render flow, return vignette("getting-started", package = "typstR").

what: Publication-Ready Scientific Documents with Quarto and Typst
title: Publication-Ready Scientific Documents with Quarto and Typst
text: typstR gives research policy teams working-paper-first path Quarto source polished Typst PDF. package ships scaffold helpers, publication metadata helpers, bundled templates can stay normal Quarto workflow using publication-ready format.

title: Publication-Ready Scientific Documents with Quarto and Typst
text: Install package R: can install typstR immediately R. Quarto required render document. default onboarding path : single working-paper flow canonical first run package.

title: Publication-Ready Scientific Documents with Quarto and Typst
text: create_working_paper() creates four key pieces: template.qmd manuscript body YAML front matter _quarto.yml project-level Quarto settings references.bib citations _extensions/typstR/ bundled format extension want full walkthrough files fit together, start Getting started.

title: Publication-Ready Scientific Documents with Quarto and Typst
text: working-paper path clear, can branch adjacent formats: Use create_article() article-style manuscripts create_policy_brief() shorter policy-facing outputs.

what: Create an affiliation metadata object — affiliation
title: Create an affiliation metadata object — affiliation
text: Returns S3 object class typstR_affiliation representing institutional affiliation. Fields serializable Quarto-compatible YAML via yaml::.yaml().

title: Create an affiliation metadata object — affiliation
code: affiliation(id, name, department = NULL, address = NULL, country = NULL)

title: Create an affiliation metadata object — affiliation
text: id Character scalar. Unique identifier used link authors affiliation (required). name Character scalar. Institution name (required). department Character scalar department name, NULL. address Character scalar address, NULL. country Character scalar country name, NULL.

title: Create an affiliation metadata object — affiliation
text: S3 object class c("typstR_affiliation", "list").

title: Create an affiliation metadata object — affiliation
code: affiliation(   id = "econ",   name = "Institute for Advanced Policy Studies",   country = "Austria" ) #> <typstR_affiliation> [econ] Institute for Advanced Policy Studies

what: Emit a formatted appendix section title — appendix_title
title: Emit a formatted appendix section title — appendix_title
text: Outputs markdown header Quarto's {.appendix} class via cat(), suitable use Quarto code chunks #| output: asis. marks section appendix Quarto's cross-reference system.

title: Emit a formatted appendix section title — appendix_title
Emit a formatted appendix section title — appendix_title
text: title single character string giving appendix section title. level integer giving header level (number # characters). Defaults 1 (top-level # header).

title: Emit a formatted appendix section title — appendix_title
text: title string, invisibly.

title: Emit a formatted appendix section title — appendix_title
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # In a Quarto code chunk with #| output: asis appendix_title("Data Sources") appendix_title("Additional Tables", level = 2) } # }

what: Create an author metadata object — author
title: Create an author metadata object — author
text: Returns S3 object class typstR_author representing manuscript author. Fields serializable Quarto-compatible YAML via yaml::.yaml().

title: Create an author metadata object — author
code: author(   name,   affiliation = NULL,   email = NULL,   orcid = NULL,   corresponding = FALSE )

title: Create an author metadata object — author
text: name Character scalar. Author's full name (required). affiliation Character vector affiliation IDs, NULL. email Character scalar email address, NULL. orcid Character scalar ORCID identifier XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX format, NULL. corresponding Logical scalar. Whether author corresponding author. Defaults FALSE.

title: Create an author metadata object — author
text: S3 object class c("typstR_author", "list").

title: Create an author metadata object — author
code: author(   name = "Ada Lovelace",   affiliation = "econ",   email = "ada@example.org",   corresponding = TRUE ) #> <typstR_author> Ada Lovelace

title: Create an article project — create_article
text: Scaffolds new article project directory Quarto document, bibliography, project configuration, typstR Quarto extension. article format identical working paper layout include report number block disclaimer page default. supports anonymized mode via typstR: anonymized: true YAML front matter.

title: Create an article project — create_article
Create an article project — create_article
text: path Path new project directory. Must already exist. title Optional title pre-fill template YAML. open Whether open project directory. Defaults TRUE interactive sessions.

title: Create an article project — create_article
text: project path, invisibly.

title: Create an article project — create_article
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ create_article(   "my-article",   title = "Firm Dynamics in Open Economies",   open = FALSE ) } # }

what: Create a policy brief project — create_policy_brief
title: Create a policy brief project — create_policy_brief
text: Scaffolds new policy brief project directory Quarto document, bibliography, project configuration, typstR Quarto extension. policy brief format uses shorter, audience-oriented layout concise summary instead full abstract. include JEL codes uses policy-oriented section headings (Key Findings, Evidence, Policy Implications).

what: Create a manuscript metadata object — manuscript_meta
title: Create a manuscript metadata object — manuscript_meta
text: Combines authors, affiliations, optional metadata fields single serializable object. Performs cross-validation: every affiliation ID referenced author must matching affiliation() object.

title: Create a manuscript metadata object — manuscript_meta
code: manuscript_meta(   authors,   affiliations = list(),   keywords = NULL,   jel = NULL,   acknowledgements = NULL,   report_number = NULL,   funding = NULL,   data_availability = NULL,   code_availability = NULL )

title: Create a manuscript metadata object — manuscript_meta
text: authors list typstR_author objects created author(). affiliations list typstR_affiliation objects created affiliation(). Defaults empty list. keywords Character vector keywords, NULL. jel Character vector JEL classification codes, NULL. acknowledgements Character scalar acknowledgement text, NULL. report_number Character scalar report number, NULL. funding Character scalar funding statement, NULL. data_availability Character scalar data availability statement, NULL. code_availability Character scalar code availability statement, NULL.

title: Create a manuscript metadata object — manuscript_meta
text: S3 object class c("typstR_meta", "list").

title: Create a manuscript metadata object — manuscript_meta
code: authors <- list(   author(     name = "Ada Lovelace",     affiliation = "econ",     corresponding = TRUE   ) )  affiliations <- list(   affiliation(     id = "econ",     name = "Institute for Advanced Policy Studies",     country = "Austria"   ) )  manuscript_meta(   authors = authors,   affiliations = affiliations,   keywords = keywords("trade", "policy"),   jel = jel_codes("F10"),   report_number = report_number("WP 001") ) #> <typstR_meta> 1 author(s), 1 affiliation(s)

typstR 1.2
text: Polished documentation surface around one onboarding-first story spanning README, pkgdown home page, articles. Added stricter exported-reference audit incomplete help pages caught regenerated Rd files ship. Started pkgdown/news/build-ignore foundation needed documentation website polish milestone.

title: typstR 1.1
text: Added structured diagnostics stable codes, ordering, payload fields validation render failures. Introduced pre-render environment validation explicit Quarto, Typst, version-floor, extension checks. Locked first-run scaffold reliability across working paper, article, policy brief formats, added measured performance regression coverage. Closed milestone supported-environment evidence audit-complete validation artifacts.

title: typstR 1.0
text: Shipped initial typstR package working paper, article, policy brief scaffolds plus Typst-backed Quarto output formats. Added manuscript metadata helpers, publication helpers, YAML-driven template integration research documents. Hardened package tests, starter documentation, CRAN-ready packaging conventions.
